title: Two Men Left the Bunker
description: Szymanski observed two German soldiers leaving the WN-71 position through its rear entrance at approximately 0645 and moving toward the draw's interior. This suggests the bunker crew has been reduced — possibly to two men operating the MG-42 rather than the standard four-man section. An undermanned crew means slower ammunition changes, reduced observation, and a longer gap between bursts. This detail, combined with the dead zone intelligence, transforms the wire push from suicidal to merely extremely dangerous.
